---
subtitle: "A Massive Template Demonstrating Every Researched Capability"
author: "Quantum Info Study Team"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    number-sections: false
    unnumbered: true
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
    theme:
      light: cosmo
      dark: darkly
    css: styles.css
    smooth-scroll: true
    anchor-sections: true
    html-math-method: katex
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
bibliography: references.bib
---

# Quarto Extended Features Showcase {.unnumbered}

# Introduction

This document serves as a "Mega-Template" that demonstrates all the advanced features of Quarto discovered during research. You can use this as a reference or a copy-paste source for building high-quality technical documents.

# Layout & Structure

## Tabs & Panels

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Python
```python
print("Hello from Python")
```

### Mermaid
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Start] --> B[End]
```

### Explanation
You can use tabsets to provide multiple views of the same concept (e.g., Code vs Theory).

:::

## Grid Layout

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-6}
### Left Column
Flexible grid system allows side-by-side content.

:::
::: {.g-col-6}
This is useful for comparing definitions or steps.

:::
:::

## Margin Content

This paragraph has a margin note. [This is a margin note that stays out of the main text flow.]{.aside}

::: {.column-margin}
### Margin Figure
```{python}
#| label: fig-margin
#| fig-cap: "A plot in the margin."
#| fig-width: 3.5
#| fig-height: 3
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
plt.plot(np.random.randn(10))
plt.show()
```

:::

# Mathematics & Science

## Equations & Cross-Ref

Here is a fundamental equation of quantum states:

$$
\rho = \sum_i p_i |\psi_i\rangle\langle\psi_i|
$$ {#eq-density}

As seen in @eq-density, density operators are convex combinations of pure states.

## Theorems & Proofs

::: {#thm-unitary}
Every closed quantum system evolves according to a unitary operator $U$.

:::

::: {.proof}
1. Consider the Schrödinger equation.
2. The solution is $e^{-iHt/\hbar}$, which is unitary.
3. Thus, $|\psi(t)\rangle = U|\psi(0)\rangle$.

:::

# Visualizations

## Mermaid Diagrams

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-complex-flow
%%| fig-cap: "Complex workflow integration."
graph TD
    A[Research] --> B{Design}
    B -- Yes --> C[Implement]
    B -- No --> A
    C --> D[Verify]
```

## Sub-figures Layout

::: {#fig-comparison layout-ncol=2}
```{python}
#| label: fig-sin
#| fig-cap: "Sine"
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
x = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi)
plt.plot(x, np.sin(x))
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cos
#| fig-cap: "Cosine"
plt.plot(x, np.cos(x))
plt.show()
```

Comparison of trigonometric functions.

:::

# Code Execution & Annotations

```{python}
import numpy as np
def quantum_gate(state):
    H = 1/np.sqrt(2) * np.array([[1, 1], [1, -1]]) # <1>
    return H @ state # <2>

initial_state = np.array([1, 0])
result = quantum_gate(initial_state)
print(f"Resulting state: {result}")
```
1. Define the Hadamard gate matrix.
2. Perform matrix multiplication with the state vector.

# Interactive Elements

## Callouts Showcase

::: {.callout-note}
Standard note.

:::

::: {.callout-tip}
Useful tip for better performance.

:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Warning about common pitfalls.

:::

::: {.callout-caution}
High-risk operation caution.

:::

::: {.callout-important}
Essential requirement details.

:::

## Custom Styles (Physics & Points)
このセクションでは、`styles.css` に定義したプロジェクト独自のカスタムコールアウトを表示します。

::: {.physics-notes title="Physics Perspective"}
In standard physics textbooks, this is often written using Ket notation as $| \psi \rangle$.

:::

::: {.important-point}
The trace of a density operator must always be 1 for probability conservation.

:::

# References & Citation

You can cite papers like this [@shannon1948].

# Appendix {.appendix}

## Additional Snippets
More detailed info goes here.
